Package Functions

java::lang::Runnable Interface Reference

Inheritance diagram for java::lang::Runnable:

Package Functions

void run ()

Detailed Description

Runnable is an interface you implement to indicate that your class can be executed as the main part of a Thread, among other places. When you want an entry point to run a piece of code, implement this interface and override run.

Author:
Paul Fisher
Tom Tromey (tromey@cygnus.com)
See also:
Thread

Definition at line 51 of file Runnable.java.

Member Function Documentation

void java::lang::Runnable::run (  )  [package]

This method will be called by whoever wishes to run your class implementing Runnable. Note that there are no restrictions on what you are allowed to do in the run method, except that you cannot throw a checked exception.

Implemented in java::lang::Thread, and jelatine::VMFinalizer.

Referenced by java::lang::Thread::run().
